summary: In patients with Down syndrome, molars exhibit severe periodontitis due to an association with taurodontism, a dental anomaly where the pulp chamber enlarges and the furcation shifts apically. This makes molars especially vulnerable to aggressive periodontal destruction compared to other tooth types.
